Article Title: 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner
Article Text:
1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner





NEW DELHI: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has appointed 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar as the new Commissioner of Police for Delhi. Kumar was currently posted as special director ( intelligence bureau ).According to an official order issued on Friday, Kumar will assume the top post in the capital's police force with effect from the date he assumes charge. He is slated to hold the position until further orders are issued.He succeeds the outgoing Commissioner, Satish Golcha, a 1992-batch IPS officer from the AGMUT cadre, who has been directed to report to the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi for a subsequent posting.This transition will take full effect upon the assumption of charge by the new incumbent, Kumar.
Article Summary: 1994-batch IPS officer Anurag Kumar replaces Satish Golcha as new Delhi Police commissioner. Kumar was currently posted as special director ( intelligence bureau) He succeeds the outgoing Commissioner, Satish. Golcha, who has been directed to report to the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi for a subsequent posting.

Article Title: EC can delete doubtful voters, cannot decide citizenship: SC
Article Text:
Only 1% of SIR appeals decided, welfare benefits denied, SC told



NEW DELHI: Supreme Court on Friday sought responses from West Bengal and Election Commission to a PIL which alleged that the state govt was denying benefits of social welfare schemes, including ration, to people whose names were deleted from the voter list because of doubtful citizenship during special intensive revision (SIR).On a PIL by Prasenjit Bose, chairperson of WB Pradesh Congress Committee and convenor of Joint Forum against NRC, a bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana said EC had the constitutional power to delete a person's name from the voter list if his/her citizenship was doubtful."But EC cannot determine whether a person is a citizen of India or not. While dealing with SIR in Bihar and West Bengal, we had repeatedly said that EC is not the authority to decide a person's citizenship status," the bench said."We had told the EC to forward the list of people with doubtful citizenship credentials to the Union govt, which is the competent authority to determine citizenship of a person," the bench said.Appearing for the petitioner, senior advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan said that as many as 33 lakh people have filed appeals against the EC decision to delete their names from the voter list. Appellate tribunals have so far disposed of 30,000 appeals of which 70% were allowed, which means their names have to be included in the voter list, he said.However, he said the BJP govt was equating the EC decision to delete people's names from the voter list as final adjudication of citizenship and depriving them of receiving benefits under welfare schemes - foodgrain under the public distribution system and the Annapurna Yojana. The state govt had earlier clarified that people whose names were deleted from the voter list after SIR but had filed appeals before the tribunals would continue to receive ration and benefits under the Annapurna scheme. Bringing another contentious issue to the court's notice, the petitioner said the state govt had issued an order asking authorities to reverify caste certificates issued to people and their dependants whose names were deleted from the voter list during SIR, for possible cancellation.Quoting a TOI report of July 2, the petitioner said only 1% of appeals have so far been adjudicated by the appellate tribunals and apprehended that it could be an unending exercise that could deprive many of welfare scheme benefits. The bench said speedy adjudication of appeals must be pleaded before the Calcutta HC chief justice. However, it entertained the plea on other issues and posted the matter for further hearing on Aug 25.
Article Summary: Supreme Court seeks responses from West Bengal and Election Commission to a PIL. PIL alleged that state govt was denying benefits of social welfare schemes, including ration, to people whose names were deleted from the voter list because of doubtful citizenship during special intensive revision (SIR)

Article Title: DMK open to talks on delimitation bill 'if Tamil Nadu interests not hurt'
Article Text: NEW DELHI: In a likely breakthrough in govt's attempts to muster a two-thirds majority in Parliament to pass the Constitution amendment bill to increase seats in Lok Sabha and implement women's reservation law from 2029, DMK , which has 22 members in Lok Sabha, said it is open to negotiation if govt can guarantee that the interests of Tamil Nadu and other southern states will not be compromised due to delimitation and if the respective share of states are spelt out in advance."Govt should explain the concrete share of each state to rule out any possibility of the southern states losing out and that can be the basis for talks," a senior DMK functionary told TOI."No formal consultation with govt has happened yet. We had conveyed even last time that we can reconsider if there is an ironclad guarantee there will be no discrimination," the functionary said.DMK's willingness to reconsider its stand may potentially turn out to be a boost for govt's efforts to secure the passage of the Constitution amendment bill.Signals from DMK and NCP-SP on the Constitution amendment bill, which will increase seats in Lok Sabha and implement women's reservation law from 2029, suggest that political momentum is building up in govt's favour. This comes on the back of the disintegration of Trinamool Congress after BJP trounced it in West Bengal elections, and the split in Sena UBT.During the debate over the two bills in the last session, the govt had unsuccessfully tried to win over DMK by offering to write into the bill the commitment that there would be a 50% increase in the representation of all states in Lok Sabha.The bill had received support of 298 MPs in April, and govt is likely to incorporate changes in its draft to assure fence-sitters, including a declaration in writing of home minister Amit Shah's oral assertion: seats in every state will go up by 50%.DMK's willingness to revisit its stand coincides with a similar indication from Sharad Pawar-led NCP, which has eight MPs.Numbers are positively stacked for govt in Rajya Sabha as well.While govt has made no public statements on its intention to bring back the bill, its top functionaries have made it clear they will revive it once they are sure of the numbers.Asked about Congress cautioning DMK on going against the bloc's decision to vote against the bill in the last session, sources in the Dravidian party accused their former ally of betrayal. "Congress destroyed INDIA bloc. They betrayed DMK by allying with Vijay-led TVK after winning five seats in the assembly polls with our support. They should stop preaching to us," a source said.While DMK sources caveated their willingness to support a delimitation plan that meets their concerns, they were unambiguous in declaring that the party, unlike in the last session, will not follow Congress' stand."Congress should disabuse itself of the notion that they are the custodian of opposition space," a DMK functionary said.
Article Summary: Signals from DMK and NCP-SP on Constitution amendment bill suggest that political momentum is building up in govt's favour. This comes on the back of the disintegration of Trinamool Congress after BJP trounced it in West Bengal elections, and the split in Sena UBT.

Article Title: Congress to join NC’s Jantar Mantar protest on Monday, BJP plans secretariat gherao
Article Text: SRINAGAR: Politics is heating up in J&K ahead of NC’s proposed protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi on July 20 over restoration of statehood, with Congress Friday announcing it would join the sit-in.BJP has planned a gherao of the secretariat in Srinagar on the same day to protest the NC govt’s “corruption, arbitrary governance, and policies that undermine democratic institutions”.“We have to go there and make our voices heard,” said Congress’s GA Mir, adding that the protest would be held even if permission was denied. “Delhi does not belong to anyone. We will enter from anywhere,” he added.Peoples Conference chief Sajad Lone, however, questioned the timing and motive behind the protest. “Major political decisions in a democracy require consensus, not unilateral calls to the street,” he said, insisting that the NC govt should instead call a special assembly session, pass a resolution on statehood, and then send an all-party delegation to the PM, before taking the protest route.Lone alleged the protest might not really be about statehood at all but about quietly burying the demand for Articles 370 and 35A.
Article Summary: BJP has planned a gherao of the secretariat in Srinagar on the same day to protest the NC govt’s “corruption, arbitrary governance, and policies that undermine democratic institutions” “We have to go there and make our voices heard,” said Congress’S GA Mir, adding that the protest would be held even if permission was denied.Peoples Conference chief Sajad Lone questioned the timing and motive behind the protest.

Article Title: No doctor would refuse treatment to brutally sexually assaulted child: Supreme Court
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court Friday warned two Ghaziabad private hospitals and a doctor that if they failed to voluntarily pay compensation to parents of a grievously injured sexually assaulted girl, who died due to refusal of treatment, it will impose costs that would have a chilling impact.When a doctor, accused of refusing admission to the child taken to a private hospital in the NCR city, pleaded that he was only a BAMS doctor and had no wherewithal to treat such a patient, a bench of CJI Surya Kant, Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana said no doctor worth his salt would refuse treatment to a minor seriously injured because of brutal sexual assault."What kind of doctor are you? If you cannot give medical assistance to a grievously injured child, do not prefix 'Dr' before your name. If you had any sensitivity, you would have facilitated her father," the bench said.The bench said, "Though the recommendation of the SIT is to impose penalty on the hospitals, we had asked you to donate. If we impose a penalty, it will have a chilling effect."On June 16, the SC asked Khajan Singh Manvi Health Care and St Joseph Hospital whether they would voluntarily compensate her parents or await court determination of the quantum. The SC-constituted SIT found credence in the father's charge that the private hospitals had refused to provide immediate treatment. The SC bench said, "The petition... highlights the alleged approach of two private hospitals and the officers of the local police station (Nandgram), which is completely indifferent, inhuman, and insensitive. "
Article Summary: The Supreme Court Friday warned two Ghaziabad private hospitals and a doctor that if they failed to voluntarily pay compensation to parents of a grievously injured sexually assaulted girl, it would impose costs that would have a chilling impact. A doctor, accused of refusing admission to the child taken to a private hospital in the NCR city, pleaded that he was only a BAMS doctor and had no wherewithal to treat such a patient.

Article Title: Unlike last time, DMK is clear it won’t follow Congress
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Signals from DMK and NCP (SP) on the Constitution amendment bill, which will increase seats in Lok Sabha and implement women’s reservation law from 2029, suggest that political momentum is building up in govt’s favour. This comes on the back of the disintegration of Trinamool Congress after BJP trounced it in Bengal elections, and the split in Shiv Sena (UBT).During the debate over the two bills in the last session, The govt had unsuccessfully tried to win over the DMK by offering to write into the bill the commitment that there would be a 50% increase in the representation of all states in Lok Sabha. The bill had received support of 298 MPs in April, and govt is likely to incorporate changes in its draft to assure fence-sitters, including a declaration in writing what home minister Amit Shah had asserted orally last time: seats in every state will go up by 50%.DMK’s willingness to revisit its stand coincides with similar indication from Sharad Pawar-led NCP, which has eight MPs. Numbers are more positively stacked for govt in Rajya Sabha as well.While govt has made no public statement on its intention to bring back the bill, its top functionaries have made it clear they will revive it once they are sure of the numbers.Asked about Congress cautioning DMK, a constituent of INDIA alliance, on going against the bloc’s decision to vote against the bill in the last session, sources in Dravidian party accused their former ally of betrayal.“Congress destroyed INDIA bloc. They betrayed DMK by allying with Vijay-led TVK after winning five seats in the assembly with our support. They should stop preaching to us,” a source said.While DMK sources caveated their willingness to support a delimitation plan that meets their concerns, they were unambiguous in declaring that the party, unlike in the last session, will not follow Congress’ stand on the issue. “Congress has a history of deceiving allies and it destroyed the United Front on flimsy ground,” said the DMK source in a reference to Congress’ decision to withdraw support from the IK Gujral-led govt in 1997 after alleging DMK had connived with the killers of former PM Rajiv Gandhi.
Article Summary: Signals from DMK and NCP (SP) on the Constitution amendment bill. Bill will increase seats in Lok Sabha and implement women’s reservation law from 2029. This comes on the back of the disintegration of Trinamool Congress after BJP trounced it in Bengal elections.

Article Title: Three ex-TMC MPs re-elected to Rajya Sabha as BJP candidates days after switching sides
Article Text:
Kolkata: BJP Rajya Sabha candidates Sukhendu Sekhar Ray, left, Sushmita Dev, centre, and Prakash Chik Baraik after receiving their election certificates. (PTI Photo)

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Former Trinamool Congress (TMC) MPs Sukhendu Sekhar Roy, Sushmita Dev, and Prakash Chik Baraik were on Friday elected unopposed to the Rajya Sabha from West Bengal as BJP members, completing a swift political turnaround days after switching sides.The three leaders were declared elected after the deadline for withdrawal of nominations expired on Friday, two days after the scrutiny of nomination papers, with no other candidates left in the fray.The trio joined the BJP in Kolkata on July 9. Hours later, the saffron party announced them as candidates for the July 24 Rajya Sabha bypolls.The bypolls were necessitated after they resigned from both the Rajya Sabha and the TMC last month following the party's defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections, creating vacancies in the three Upper House seats.The assembly election result brought the BJP to power in West Bengal for the first time, ending the TMC's 15-year rule.The former TMC MPs' election marks another setback for the party, with all three returning to the Rajya Sabha on BJP tickets within weeks of quitting the Trinamool Congress, after it split into factions led by Mamata Banerjee and Ritabrata Banerjee following its defeat in the April elections.They were also the first TMC leaders to join the BJP after the assembly election results.Roy, who represented the TMC in the Rajya Sabha since 2012, was regarded as one of the party's key legal and parliamentary strategists. His differences with the leadership became public after the RG Kar rape and murder case, when he sought accountability from his own party, drawing criticism from within the TMC.Though he later deleted some social media posts, citing threats to his family's safety, the episode further widened his rift with the leadership, sources said.Dev, a former Congress Lok Sabha MP from Assam, joined the TMC in 2021. Before switching to the BJP, she had criticised the Trinamool leadership over alleged corruption.
Article Summary: Former Trinamool Congress MPs Sukhendu Sekhar Roy, Sushmita Dev, and Prakash Chik Baraik elected unopposed to the Rajya Sabha from West Bengal as BJP members. The three leaders were declared elected after the deadline for withdrawal of nominations expired on Friday, two days after the scrutiny of nomination papers.

Article Title: 'Kattar Beimaan Party': PM Modi attacks AAP on corruption, law and order at Jalandhar rally
Article Text:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi addresses the gathering during a public meeting, in Jalandhar, Punjab. (@NarendraModi/YT via PTI Photo)

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday targeted Punjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) over several issues at a rally in Jalandhar, months ahead of the state assembly elections due early next year.Accusing AAP leaders of corruption, PM Modi called it a "Kattar Beimaan Party" (staunchly dishonest party) — a jibe at AAP national convenor and former Delhi chief minister Arvind Kejriwal's description of it as a "Kattar Imaandaar Party" (staunchly honest party)."In Punjab, the health minister was sacked over bribery allegations. Another minister faced a money laundering case. Their MLAs are being caught taking bribes. There are very few among them," the prime minister said without naming the AAP."The party ruling in Punjab is now known as Kattar Beimaan Party," he added.PM Modi further remarked that Punjab's law and order situation was in "shambles.""Law and order in Punjab is in shambles today. No one can predict when or where a gang war might break out or from which direction bullets might start flying. Even police stations are not safe as they are frequently attacked," the prime minister said, apparently referring to the February 2023 attack on a police station in Amritsar by supporters of pro-Khalistan leader Amritpal Singh.Amritpal Singh is currently lodged in a jail in Assam's Dibrugarh. He contested the 2024 Lok Sabha elections from jail and won the Khadoor Sahib seat in Punjab.Meanwhile, the PM also addressed the conviction of former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain, who was found guilty of murdering Intelligence Bureau (IB) officer Ankit Sharma during the February 2020 Delhi riots."Just 2-3 days ago, a Delhi court convicted one of their leaders. He stands accused of inciting riots and murdering an IB officer; and look at their brazenness... they are questioning the court's judgment, viewing it through the prism of religion and faith simply because their vote bank is at stake," the prime minister stated.He also appealed to voters to bring the BJP to power in the state, saying the saffron party would work towards building a "developed and prosperous" Punjab.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi targeted Punjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) over several issues at a rally in Jalandhar. Accusing AAP leaders of corruption, PM Modi called it a "Kattar Beimaan Party" (staunchly dishonest party) He also appealed to voters to bring the BJP to power in the state.

Article Title: Ram Mandir donation case: Tinnu Yadav, Manish Yadav sent to 39-hour police remand
Article Text:
Ayodhya Ram temple (PTI photo)

NEW DELHI: A court on Friday granted 39 hours of police remand for Ram Shankar Yadav alias Tinnu Yadav and Manish Yadav in connection with the alleged embezzlement of Ayodhya Ram temple's donations.The two accused are expected to be taken out of jail on Saturday for custodial interrogation as part of the ongoing investigation into the alleged donation scam, which has sparked a major political row.Earlier, Ayodhya police had sought a seven-day remand for the two accused."The police told the court that custodial interrogation is crucial to uncover the conspiracy, trace the money trail, and recover further evidence," Ayodhya Police said.On Wednesday, two other accused in the case -- Subhash Chandra Srivastava, a retired bank employee who supervised the temple's cash-counting operations, and Ramashankar Mishra -- were taken from the District Jail in Ayodhya after a court granted the police a 14-hour remand for custodial interrogation.Police officials questioned the duo as part of the probe into the alleged misappropriation of temple donations.So far, investigators have interrogated four other accused -- Avinash Shukla, Anukalp Mishra, Lavkush Mishra and Karunesh Pandey -- during their police custody remand.The alleged embezzlement of donations for the Ram Temple has triggered widespread political backlash, with opposition parties targeting the ruling BJP over the issue.
Article Summary: Court grants 39 hours police remand for Ram Shankar Yadav alias Tinnu Yadav and Manish Yadav. The two accused are expected to be taken out of jail on Saturday for custodial interrogation. The alleged embezzlement of donations for the Ram Temple has triggered widespread political backlash.

Article Title: Call all-party meet to discuss delimitation bill: Kharge to PM Modi
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think the reintroduction of the delimitation bill will favor the BJP? Yes No 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Amid reports that govt is looking to reintroduce delimitation bill in the Monsoon session, Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge Thursday wrote to PM Narendra Modi , urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill.Kharge wrote that he had repeatedly asked the parliamentary affairs minister for an all-party meeting during the Budget session, but his demand was not heeded, after which the delimitation bill fell in Lok Sabha on April 17 — a reference to the defeat of the govt bill which the opposition termed as being in name for women’s reservation but having the real objective of delimiting Lok Sabha seats on the basis of population to advantage BJP and hurt the southern states.Kharge wrote to Modi that he has been reading media reports about govt move to reintroduce the bill with some changes, urging the PM that he should share the details with the opposition and give it adequate time to study the proposal before it is introduced in Parliament.Kharge’s initiative to settle the heightened speculation over the bill came even as Congress said that despite splitting TMC and Shiv Sena, Modi govt is far from achieving the 2/3rd majority required to pass a constitutional amendment bill.AICC spokesman Jairam Ramesh said Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, and the Congress leadership are in touch with all the parties “that voted against the said bill in April”, and discounted the possibility of DMK and NCP voting in its favour on reintroduction.“The home minister is trying his best to break the parties to achieve the majority mark, but he is far from it. He is attempting to achieve a tainted majority by splitting parties, which is against the people’s mandate and the constitution,” he said.About DMK with which Congress had a bitter falling out, Ramesh said Congress is in touch with the Tamil Nadu party. “They are fundamentally opposed to the ideology of BJP and the way delimitation has been done in Assam and J&K,” Ramesh said, including SP in his remarks.
Article Summary: Mallikarjun Kharge writes to PM Narendra Modi urging him to call an all-party meeting to discuss the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill. Kharge wrote to Modi that he has been reading media reports about govt move to reintroduce the bill with some changes, urging the PM that he should share the details with the opposition.

Article Title: Senior BJP leader, former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passes away at 88
Article Text: BENGALURU: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88 at a private hospital in Bengaluru on Tuesday.A veteran leader, Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades.Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death of senior BJP leader and former minister Ramachandra Gowda, describing him as a "brother" and a longtime companion.In a post on X, Yediyurappa recalled their journey together since the Jana Sangh era, their efforts to build the party in Karnataka, and praised Gowda's administrative acumen and dedication as a minister.BS Yediyurappa tweets, "I have lost yet another brother. The news of the passing of Shri Ramachandra Gowda, my soulmate of decades, senior leader, and former minister, has brought me immense sorrow. The days of struggle—standing shoulder to shoulder since the Jana Sangh era, toiling day and night, building the party from the grassroots level together in the state—swirl before my eyes. As a colleague in my cabinet, I have witnessed his administrative acumen and dedication up close. "
Article Summary: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88. Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades. Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death.

Article Title: Delhi News - The Times of India
Article Text: New Delhi: A lab assistant has been arrested Friday for allegedly sexually assaulting a class VI student at a govt school in north east Delhi’s Seemapuri. Acting on the student’s complaint, police registered a case under sections related to molestation and Pocso act , and arrested the accused.In her complaint to the police, the victim stated that she had visited the school’s science lab in May when the incident happened. Further probe is underway.
Article Summary: A lab assistant has been arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting a class VI student at a govt school in north east Delhi’s Seemapuri. Police registered a case under sections related to molestation and Pocso act and arrested the accused.

Article Title: HC stays Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar town vending committee polls
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has stayed the election programme for the town vending committee (TVC) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, observing that there appeared to be prima facie merit in the contention that the labour commissioner could not delegate the power to conduct the poll to the municipal commissioner under the Maharashtra Street Vendors (Protection of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Rules, 2016.A division bench of Justices Nitin B Suryawanshi and Abasaheb D Shinde passed the interim order on Friday while hearing a writ petition filed by the Shahid Bhagatsingh Hawkers’ Union through its president, advocate Abhay Manohar Taksal, who appeared as party-in-person. The court issued notices to the respondents and scheduled the next hearing for Aug 13.The petition challenges the election programme published on June 29 for the constitution of the TVC. According to the petitioner, Rule 14 of the 2016 Rules permits the labour commissioner to delegate poll-related powers only to a subordinate officer not below the rank of labour officer and not to the commissioner of a municipal corporation.Taking note of the submission, the bench observed that the contention appeared to have merit and ordered, “Until further orders, there shall be stay to the election programme published on 29/06/2026.”The election process had been initiated by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Municipal Corporation (CSMC), which had announced polling on July 25. The civic body had proposed voting at six municipal schools via 20 polling booths to elect eight members of the town vending committee.
Article Summary: Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has stayed the election programme for the town vending committee (TVC) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. A division bench of Justices Nitin B Suryawanshi and Abasaheb D Shinde passed the interim order on Friday while hearing a writ petition filed by the Shahid Bhagatsingh Hawkers’ Union.

Article Title: AAP holds funeral procession of a bike against ethanol-blended petrol in Kolhapur
Article Text: Aam Aadmi Party members hold a symbolic funeral procession for a two-wheeler in Kolhapur on Thursday to protest the mandatory sale of ethanol-blended petrol
Article Summary: Aam Aadmi Party members hold a symbolic funeral procession for a two-wheeler in Kolhapur on Thursday to protest the mandatory sale of ethanol-blended petrol. The funeral procession was led by a member of the party's state unit. The party is demanding a ban on the use of ethanol in petrol.

Article Title: 7 FIRs filed for theft of 1,750kg copper coils from MSEDCL transformers in Panvel
Article Text:
The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. (AI-generated image)

NAVI MUMBAI: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh from MSEDCL transformers kept at different plots in Karanjade node of Panvel taluka. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.According to the complainant, Amit Palve (42), assistant engineer with MSEDCL's Pargaon branch in Panvel taluka, the thieves broke open the windows of rooms at seven different plots where the defunct transformers were kept and stole the copper coils from them.When contacted, Palve said, "The seven transformers were defunct and hence kept in the rooms at the respective plots. On July 6, the theft came to light and we informed the Panvel City Police, who carried out a preliminary investigation. As there was no CCTV surveillance in the area, there was no technical evidence. Since it was a major theft, an FIR was registered against unidentified accused under relevant BNS sections for the offences of trespassing and theft. "
Article Summary: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.

Article Title: Doctor booked for threatening man with licensed pistol
Article Text:
Pune:

The Hadapsar police on Friday registered a case against a medical practitioner attached to a private hospital for allegedly threatening a deceased patient’s relative’s friend with a licensed pistol during a dispute over a hospital bill.The incident took place on the hospital premises on Thursday night when the complainant visited the doctor’s cabin seeking a concession in the treatment bill.Police sub-inspector Ratnadeep Birajdar said the complainant had accompanied his friend, whose mother died while undergoing treatment at the hospital earlier that day.“The complainant alleged that the doctor refused to reduce the bill and threatened him with dire consequences by brandishing his licensed firearm,” Birajdar said.The accused has been booked under the relevant provisions of the Arms Act and has been summoned for questioning, police said.
Article Summary: The incident took place on the hospital premises on Thursday night. The complainant visited the doctor’s cabin seeking a concession in the treatment bill. The accused has been booked under the relevant provisions of the Arms Act and has been summoned for questioning, police said.

Article Title: NCP state deputy chief points to some party members’ bid to end Parth’s career in letter to Sunetra
Article Text: NCP state deputy chief points to some party members’ bid to end Parth’s career in letter to Sunetra
Article Summary: NCP state deputy chief points to some party members’ bid to end Parth’s career in letter to Sunetra. Parth is a former member of the NCP state executive committee. He was sacked by the party for alleged anti-party activities.

Article Title: College student dies in road accident near Krishnagiri
Article Text:
The accident happenednear the Sendrayampatti bus stop on Friday morning





KRISHNAGIRI: A college student died after a Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation bus rammed his two-wheeler in Krishnagiri on Friday.The deceased has been identified as Madesh, 18, of N Thattakkal village near Pochampalli in Krishnagiri. He was a first-year degree student at a private college.The accident happened when Madesh was returning home from Velampatti after seeing off some relatives. As he was negotiating a narrow, sharp curve near the Sendrayampatti bus stop, a TNSTC bus coming from the opposite direction rammed his motorcycle. Madesh fell under a front wheel of the bus and was crushed to death on the spot.Upon receiving information about the accident, the Nagarasampatti police rushed to the spot, recovered Madesh’s body and sent it to the Krishnagiri Govt Medical College Hospital for postmortem. Police registered a case and began investigations.According to locals, dense tamarind trees line both sides of the road, completely obstructing the view around the narrow curve. Drivers are unable to see oncoming vehicles until it is too late, which they allege was the reason for this accident.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Madesh, 18, of N Thattakkal village near Pochampalli in Krishnagiri. He was a first-year degree student at a private college. The accident happened when Madesh was returning home from Velampatti after seeing off some relatives.

Article Title: SSI held by DVAC for taking ₹70,000 bribe in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





Sleuths from thedirectorate of vigilance and anti-corruption (DVAC) on Thursday arrested a special sub-inspector attached to the Kattoor police station for accepting a bribe of ₹70,000.The arrested has been identified as Boopathi. He was arrested while receiving the bribe near the Ukkadam bus stand on Thursday evening.According to DVAC officials, the complainant, Rujaina Fathima of Tenkasi, had mortgaged her husband’s off-road sports utility vehicle to Ravikumar of Tirunelveli and received ₹6 lakh from him. They had agreed that the vehicle would be returned once Fathima repaid the amount.A week ago, when Ravikumar visited Coimbatore in the vehicle. Fathima and her husband tracked the SUV using the GPS device fitted in it, came to Coimbatore and took the vehicle using a spare key. They later parked it at a parking facility in Pollachi.Ravikumar then lodged a complaint with Kattoor police and a case was registered. Police subsequently secured the vehicle and brought Fathima to the station for inquiry. They also took her mobile phones, citing investigation. Fathima subsequently settled the amount with Ravikumar and reclaimed the vehicle.However, Boopathi allegedly contacted her and demanded ₹70,000 to close the case and return her mobile phones.Unwilling to pay the bribe, Fathima approached DVAC officials on Wednesday evening, following which they handed over chemical coated currency notes to her.On Thursday evening, Fathima met Boopathi near the Ukkadam bus stand and handed over the money. DVAC sleuths, who were waiting nearby, caught Boopathi red-handed after he accepted the bribe and arrested him.DVAC officials have registered a case and are investigating further.
Article Summary: Sleuths from thedirectorate of vigilance and anti-corruption (DVAC) on Thursday arrested a special sub-inspector attached to the Kattoor police station. The arrested has been identified as Boopathi. He was arrested while receiving the bribe near the Ukkadam bus stand on Thursday evening.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Palani land regn: Sub-registrar gets interim advance bail
Article Text: Madurai: Madras high court on Friday granted interim anticipatory bail to suspended sub-registrar Justin Manikandan Subramanian who is apprehending arrest in the case pertaining to fraudulent registration of the 1.4-acre land belonging to Arulmigu Dhandapani Swamigal mutt in Palani.Senior counsel for petitioner submitted that the entire registration process including signing of document was done by the previous sub-registrar Balasundar on July 3. The petitioner, who was sent on deputation, had just assigned registration number for the document on July 6. It was done only for the purpose of impounding the document since it was undervalued, and to refer it to the collector (stamps) under Section 47-A of Indian Stamp Act.However, the state submitted that district registrar Sasikala, who sent the petitioner on deputation, was also suspended. A strong suspicion arose on the petitioner’s role. The larger conspiracy behind the registration of the document was still under investigation. There were two previous cases of similar nature against the petitioner and his custodial interrogation was necessary, the state said while opposing anticipatory bail.Justice K Rajasekar observed that the allegation against the petitioner is that by assigning the registration number, he completed the process of registration. The prosecution has taken a specific stand that the larger conspiracy surrounding the registration of the mutt property is still under investigation and that the role of each of the accused is yet to be ascertained. Since the FIR was registered recently, the court is of the view that investigation should not be impeded.In order to balance the interests of the investigation and the liberty of the petitioner, the judge granted interim anticipatory bail to the petitioner on certain conditions. The petitioner shall appear before Dindigul CB-CID police everyday at 10.30am from July 27 to Aug 3, except Sunday, and co-operate with the investigation. The matter was posted for further hearing on August 4.Meanwhile, a CB-CID team led by SP B H Sajitha continued inquiry in the case for the second day on Friday. More than five people, including HR and CE department superintendent Muruganandam were questioned in connection with the alleged fraudulent land registration. Palani tahsildar Sanjay Gandhi appeared before the CB-CID for inquiry.Officials are examining revenue and temple records to ascertain how the temple land was allegedly registered illegally. Simultaneously, another five-member CB-CID team carried out inquiries at Palani sub-registrar office. The officials took possession of certain original documents and files related to the disputed registration, said sources.
Article Summary: Sub-registrar Justin Manikandan Subramanian is apprehending arrest in the case pertaining to fraudulent registration of the 1.4-acre land belonging to Arulmigu Dhandapani Swamigal mutt in Palani.

Article Title: Karti backs DMK-TVK cooperation at nat’l level
Article Text:
Congress





Madurai: Congress MP Karti Chidambaram has backed VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan’s suggestion that DMK and TVK could cooperate at the national level despite being rivals in Tamil Nadu.“Parties can have competing interests in a state, but when it comes to national politics—and if they are committed to secular politics—there can be cooperation,” Karti told reporters in Madurai. He cited Kerala, where Congress and CPM contest against each other in the state but work together on issues in Parliament.“But that requires a certain amount of political maturity,” he said. He also referred to West Bengal, where Congress and TMC had competed electorally but cooperated on parliamentary matters.Asked about former BJP leader K Annamalai quitting the party and launching a movement, Karti said, “Good luck to him. I mean, he has realised that the BJP will not take him very far.” He rejected suggestions that Annamalai’s movement could function as a BJP proxy in Tamil Nadu. “No, I think BJP has no team in Tamil Nadu. They don’t have A team, B team or Z team. BJP is a rejected team in Tamil Nadu,” he added.
Article Summary: Congress MP Karti Chidambaram has backed VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan’s suggestion that DMK and TVK could cooperate at the national level despite being rivals in Tamil Nadu. He cited Kerala, where Congress and CPM contest against each other in the state but work together on issues in Parliament.

Article Title: Woman found dead with her throat slit in Virudhunagar district
Article Text:
Woman found dead with her throat slit in Virudhunagar district





Madurai: A 31-year-old woman was found dead with her throat slit at her house near Kariapatti in Virudhunagar district on Thursday.Police identified the deceased as Mayil of Keela Azhagiyanallur village. She was married to Muniyasamy, a construction worker, and the couple has two children. On Thursday morning, Mayil was alone at the house after her husband left for work and the children went to school.During the evening, when her son studying Class V came home, he found his mother lying dead in a pool of blood with her throat slit. She was found holding a knife in her hand.On information, police rushed to the spot and sent her body to the Virudhunagar govt medical college hospital for postmortem. Mallankinaru police registered a case and are investigating.
Article Summary: Police identified the deceased as Mayil of Keela Azhagiyanallur village. She was married to Muniyasamy, a construction worker, and the couple has two children. Mayil was alone at the house after her husband left for work.

Article Title: YouTuber arrested again for insulting Sreenadevi Kunjamma on social media
Article Text:
Kottayam

:

An Adoor court on Friday remanded YouTuber Rajan Joseph, 48, who was arrested again for posting derogatory content on social media targeting Pathanamthitta district panchayat member and Congress functionary Sreenadevi Kunjamma. Joseph, a native of Manjeri in Malappuram district, had earlier been granted bail in a similar case involving alleged derogatory remarks against Kunjamma.He was taken into custody from Kochi on Thursday night.In the first case, Adoor police had registered a case on June 4. He was arrested last Sunday and was produced in a court. Subsequently, the court granted him bail. However, that same evening, Joseph again made a video insulting Kunjamma and posted it on social media. Following it, Kunjamma again approached the police with a complaint.It was in Oct 2025 that Joseph first posted videos about Kunjamma on social media. In addition to the charge of circulating videos that insult womanhood, this time Joseph was arrested on charges under the IT Act as well.
Article Summary: Rajan Joseph, 48, was arrested again for posting derogatory content on social media targeting Pathanamthitta district panchayat member and Congress functionary Sreenadevi Kunjamma. Joseph, a native of Manjeri in Malappuram district, had earlier been granted bail in a similar case.

Article Title: Yellow alert in north Kerala for next 2 days
Article Text: Yellow alert in north Kerala for next 2 days
Article Summary: Yellow alert in north Kerala for next 2 days. Yellow alert is a state-wide alert that warns people to stay indoors. The alert is in place for the next two days, authorities say. It is the second such alert in the state in two days.

Article Title: Sexual assault: Kerala HC upholds order directing FIR against three police officers
Article Text:
Kochi

:

High court has dismissed a petition filed by three senior police officers, including a superintendent of police, seeking to quash a magistrate’s order directing an investigation into allegations of rape and sexual assault levelled against them by a woman.A bench of Justice Jobin Sebastian on Friday upheld the judicial first-class magistrate court, Ponnani’s decision to forward the private complaint for investigation by dismissing a petition filed by the three police officers. The petitioners, Vinod Valiyatoor, former station house officer (SHO) of Ponnani police station, V V Benny, deputy superintendent of police and S Sujith Das, former superintendent of police, Malappuram, are accused of repeatedly sexually assaulting the complainant in 2022 after she approached them for assistance in connection with a property dispute. The allegations include rape, molestation and criminal intimidation.The complainant alleged that Vinod visited her residence as part of an inquiry into the property dispute and sexually assaulted her. She further alleged that Benny and Das also sexually assaulted her on separate occasions. Despite lodging complaints with police, no FIR was registered. Subsequently, following HC’s intervention, the magistrate forwarded the private complaint to the police and directed an investigation under Section 175(3) of BNSS.Aggrieved by that order, the accused officers moved HC.The petitioners contended that the magistrate had failed to comply with the procedural safeguard under Section 175(4) of the BNSS, which requires obtaining a report from a superior police officer before directing an investigation against a public servant for acts allegedly committed while discharging official duties. They argued that the allegations arose out of acts performed in the course of their official duties. Rejecting the contention, HC held that the protection under Section 175(4) applies only where the alleged act has a reasonable nexus with the discharge of official duties. Acts such as rape and sexual assault are wholly alien to the discharge of official duties and, therefore, do not attract the statutory protection, HC observed.HC also rejected the contention that the complaint was not supported by a proper affidavit, holding that an affidavit sworn before an advocate, as permitted under the Kerala Criminal Rules of Practice, fulfils the legislative object of preventing frivolous complaints. Finding no ground for interference, HC dismissed the petition, thereby allowing the investigation to proceed.
Article Summary: High court dismisses petition filed by three senior police officers seeking to quash a magistrate’s order directing an investigation into allegations of rape and sexual assault levelled against them. The petitioners, Vinod Valiyatoor, former station house officer (SHO) of Ponnani police station, V V Benny, deputy superintendent of police and S Sujith Das are accused of repeatedly sexually assaulting the complainant.
